texto: In early 1970, Newsweek's editors decided that the new women's liberation movement deserved a cover story. There was one problem, however: there were no women to write the piece.
explicacao: Citação de Lynn Povich sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “In early 1970, Newsweek's editors decided that the new women's liberation movement deserved a cov…” articula reflexao, discrimination, feminism em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como feminism e journalism.

frase_original: In early 1970, Newsweek's editors decided that the new women's liberation movement deserved a cover story. There was one problem, however: there were no women to write the piece.

texto: There were elements of Mad Men at Newsweek, except that unlike the natty advertising types, journalists were notorious slobs and our two- and three-martini lunches were out of the office, not in...Kevin Buckley, who was hired in 1963, described the Newsweek of the early 1960s as similar to an old movie, with the wisecracking private eye and his Girl Friday. "The 'hubba-hubba' climate was tolerated," he recalled. "I was told the editors would ask the girls to do handstands on their desk. Was there rancor? Yes. But in this climate, a laugh would follow.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Lynn Povich que provoca temas ligados a trabalho. Ao questionar “There were elements of Mad Men at Newsweek, except that unlike the natty advertising types, journ…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre trabalho, 1960s, girl-friday, com foco em trabalho e girl-friday.

frase_original: There were elements of Mad Men at Newsweek, except that unlike the natty advertising types, journalists were notorious slobs and our two- and three-martini lunches were out of the office, not in...Kevin Buckley, who was hired in 1963, described the Newsweek of the early 1960s as similar to an old movie, with the wisecracking private eye and his Girl Friday. "The 'hubba-hubba' climate was tolerated," he recalled. "I was told the editors would ask the girls to do handstands on their desk. Was there rancor? Yes. But in this climate, a laugh would follow.
